perturbation.original_text: Provider and Recipient shall each be referred to herein individually, as a “ Party ” and collectively, as the “ Parties .”
perturbation.changed_text: Provider and Recipient shall each be referred to herein individually, as a “Party” and collectively, as the “Entities.”
perturbation.explanation: The original text establishes 'Party' and 'Parties' to refer to the individual and collective entities, respectively. This change replaces 'Parties' with 'Entities' when referring to them collectively. This creates an internal contradiction later on when the original term 'Parties' is used, making it unclear which collective noun is intended. If they're the same Parties or some other legal Entity.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient understands and agrees that no contract or agreement providing for a Possible Transaction exists between the Parties unless and until a final definitive agreement for a Possible Transaction has been executed and delivered, and Recipient hereby waives, in advance, any claims (including, without limitation, breach of contract) relating to the existence of a Possible Transaction unless and until the Parties shall have entered into a final definitive agreement for a Possible Transaction. Recipient also agrees that, unless and until a final definitive agreement regarding a Possible Transaction has been executed and delivered, neither of the Parties will be under any legal obligation of any kind whatsoever with respect to such Possible Transaction by virtue of this Agreement except for the matters specifically agreed to herein. None of the Parties are under any obligation to accept any proposal regarding a Possible Transaction and the Parties may terminate discussions and negotiations at any time.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Parties' with 'Entities'. The contradicted text uses the original term 'Parties' multiple times. This creates a direct contradiction, making it unclear whether 'Parties' in the contradicted text refers to the same entities now referred to as 'Entities', or if it refers to some other grouping, creating uncertainty and potentially rendering the contradicted text unenforceable. This adheres to the definition of Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: In connection with the consideration of a possible negotiated transaction (a “ Possible Transaction ”) between the Parties, Provider is prepared to make available to Recipient certain “Evaluation Material” (as defined in Section 2 below) in accordance with the provisions of this Agreement, and Recipient hereby agrees to take or abstain from taking certain other actions as hereinafter set forth.
perturbation.changed_text: In connection with the consideration of a possible negotiated transaction (a “Negotiation”) between the Parties, Provider is prepared to make available to Recipient certain “Evaluation Material” (as defined in Section 2 below) in accordance with the provisions of this Agreement, and Recipient hereby agrees to take or abstain from taking certain other actions as hereinafter set forth.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Possible Transaction" to "Negotiation" introduces ambiguity. While initially defined, later sections still refer to "Possible Transaction", creating confusion about whether they are the same thing. This could impact clauses that refer back to the "Possible Transaction" specifically, since the contract refers to both now. Leading to uncertain enforcement on the definition.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient understands and agrees that no contract or agreement providing for a Possible Transaction exists between the Parties unless and until a final definitive agreement for a Possible Transaction has been executed and delivered, and Recipient hereby waives, in advance, any claims (including, without limitation, breach of contract) relating to the existence of a Possible Transaction unless and until the Parties shall have entered into a final definitive agreement for a Possible Transaction. Recipient also agrees that, unless and until a final definitive agreement regarding a Possible Transaction has been executed and delivered, neither of the Parties will be under any legal obligation of any kind whatsoever with respect to such Possible Transaction by virtue of this Agreement except for the matters specifically agreed to herein. None of the Parties are under any obligation to accept any proposal regarding a Possible Transaction and the Parties may terminate discussions and negotiations at any time.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines "Possible Transaction", and the contradicted text refers back to this definition consistently. The changed text alters "Possible Transaction" to "Negotiation" in the initial definition. While Section 2 might still define "Evaluation Material", subsequent sections like the contradicted text continue to reference "Possible Transaction". This creates a direct contradiction and introduces ambiguity as to whether "Negotiation" and "Possible Transaction" are the same, contradicting the text that assumes 'Possible Transaction' is clearly defined and understood throughout the document. This adheres to the definition of Misaligned Terminology causing In-Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient agrees that, for a period of one year after the date of this Agreement (the “ Standstill Period ”), unless specifically invited in writing by Provider, neither it nor any of its affiliates who have been provided Evaluation Material, will in any manner, directly or indirectly: (a) effect, seek, offer or propose (whether publicly or otherwise) to effect, or cause or participate in, or in any way assist any other Person to effect, seek, offer or propose (whether publicly or otherwise) to effect or participate in: (i) any acquisition of any equity securities (or beneficial ownership thereof) or all or substantially all of the assets of Provider or any of its subsidiaries, (ii) any tender or exchange offer, merger or other business combination involving Provider or any of its subsidiaries, (iii) any recapitalization, restructuring, liquidation, dissolution or other extraordinary transaction with respect to Provider or any of its subsidiaries, or (iv) any “solicitation” of “proxies” (as such terms are used in the proxy rules of the Securities and Exchange Commission) or consents to vote any voting securities of Provider;
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient agrees that, for a period of one year after the date of this Agreement (the “Restriction Period”), unless specifically invited in writing by Provider, neither it nor any of its affiliates who have been provided Evaluation Material, will in any manner, directly or indirectly: (a) effect, seek, offer or propose (whether publicly or otherwise) to effect, or cause or participate in, or in any way assist any other Person to effect, seek, offer or propose (whether publicly or otherwise) to effect or participate in: (i) any acquisition of any equity securities (or beneficial ownership thereof) or all or substantially all of the assets of Provider or any of its subsidiaries, (ii) any tender or exchange offer, merger or other business combination involving Provider or any of its subsidiaries, (iii) any recapitalization, restructuring, liquidation, dissolution or other extraordinary transaction with respect to Provider or any of its subsidiaries, or (iv) any “solicitation” of “proxies” (as such terms are used in the proxy rules of the Securities and Exchange Commission) or consents to vote any voting securities of Provider;
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'Standstill Period' to 'Restriction Period' the term becomes inconsistent. In the following text it is referred to as 'Standstill Period' and this creates a confusing ambiguity. As the contract does not define both, but only defines one and contradicts itself later on in the text. This introduces internal contradiction within the contract that can have uncertain enforcement, leading to it being open to legal interpretability.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient also agrees during the Standstill Period not to publicly request (or request in a manner or under circumstances that would reasonably require public disclosure of such request) Provider (or its directors, officers, employees or agents), directly or indirectly, to amend or waive any provision of this Section 9 (including this sentence). Recipient further agrees that unless otherwise directed by Provider in writing (i) all communications with the Provider regarding a Possible Transaction, (ii) requests for additional information, facility tours, or management meetings, and (iii) discussions or questions regarding procedures with respect to a Possible Transaction, will be submitted or directed by Recipient or its Representatives only to Ethan Zweig of Citigroup Global Markets Inc., as Provider’s financial advisor, or a person or persons designated in writing by Mr. Zweig, unless otherwise agreed to by the Provider.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Standstill Period' with 'Restriction Period'. The contradicted text refers to 'Standstill Period'. This creates a direct contradiction and ambiguity because the contract now uses a term ('Standstill Period') that is no longer defined in the modified section, leading to inconsistency and potential unenforceability. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition provided.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient understands and acknowledges that neither Provider nor its Representatives makes any representation or warranty, express or implied, as to the accuracy or completeness of the Evaluation Material furnished by or on behalf of Provider and shall have no liability to Recipient, its Representatives or any other Person relating to or resulting from the use of the Evaluation Material furnished to Recipient or its respective Representatives or any errors therein or omissions therefrom.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient understands and acknowledges that neither Provider nor its Representatives makes any guarantee, express or implied, as to the accuracy or completeness of the Evaluation Material furnished by or on behalf of Provider and shall have no liability to Recipient, its Representatives or any other Person relating to or resulting from the use of the Evaluation Material furnished to Recipient or its respective Representatives or any errors therein or omissions therefrom.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "representation or warranty" to "guarantee" introduces confusion, even if the goal is to provide additional clarification on that phrase. In legal contexts, "representation" and "warranty" have a very specific meaning and legal weight. The term 'guarantee' may impose a stricter level of liability on Provider, contradicting the intent of the original clause. This can introduce an in-text contradiction where 'representation' and 'warranty' are later invoked as a term.
perturbation.contradicted_text: As to the information delivered to Recipient, Provider will only be liable for those representations or warranties which are made in a final definitive agreement regarding a Possible Transaction, when, as and if executed, and subject to such limitations and restrictions as may be specified therein. Nothing in this Agreement shall be construed as obligating Provider to provide, or to continue to provide, any information to any Person.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces "representation or warranty" with "guarantee". The contradicted text states that Provider will only be liable for 'those representations or warranties' made in a final agreement. By replacing 'representation or warranty' with 'guarantee' in the changed text, it creates a potential contradiction because the contradicted text only references 'representation or warranties' and not 'guarantees', which means the provider might be liable to guarantees. This inconsistency, according to the explanation, creates an in-text contradiction by misaligning terminology as per definition 3.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement is for the benefit of each of the Parties and shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Delaware applicable to agreements made and to be performed entirely within such state.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement is for the benefit of each of the Parties and shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of California applicable to agreements made and to be performed entirely within such state.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the governing law from Delaware to California introduces a contradiction because different states have different contract law interpretations and precedents. This directly contradicts the section where it states that the agreement should be construed by Delaware state law. A contract can't specify that it will be construed under 2 different state laws, leading to uncertainty in enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement is for the benefit of each of the Parties and shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of California applicable to agreements made and to be performed entirely within such state.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the governing law from Delaware to California. The contradicted text also states the governing law is California. However, the explanation states that the modification introduces a contradiction because different states have different contract law interpretations and precedents, which is an ambiguity as stated in the definition, leading to conflicting obligations, and thus contradicts the contradicted text. Therefore, the answer is YES
